title: Add burnAMMTokens support to DeployableTokenManager and prove AMM sell path
description:
Problem
The fresh Sepolia stack uses DeployableTokenManager, but HokusaiAMM.sell() calls tokenManager.burnAMMTokens(...). Legacy TokenManager implements burnAMMTokens, but DeployableTokenManager currently does not. Buys pass because mintTokens exists, but post-IBR sells can revert when the AMM calls the missing selector.

Relevant code

* contracts/HokusaiAMM.sol imports TokenManager and calls burnAMMTokens during sell.
* contracts/TokenManager.sol implements burnAMMTokens.
* contracts/DeployableTokenManager.sol implements burnTokens and burnInvestorTokens, but not burnAMMTokens.

Required fix
Add burnAMMTokens(string modelId, address account, uint256 amount) to DeployableTokenManager with the same semantics as legacy TokenManager: authorize MINTER_ROLE / owner / deltaVerifier callers, validate inputs, resolve model token, call token.burnAMM(account, amount), and emit TokensBurned.

Acceptance criteria

* DeployableTokenManager exposes burnAMMTokens with the expected selector and behavior.
* HokusaiAMM can call burnAMMTokens against DeployableTokenManager during sell.
* Local deployed-stack/e2e test uses DeployableTokenManager, buys tokens, advances past IBR or configures zero IBR, executes sell, and verifies reserve/supply/accounting invariants.
* Test covers reward/supplier token holders selling if supported by the intended economics, or explicitly documents any unsupported sell provenance.
* Sepolia e2e token suite can run a real sell after IBR or in a local equivalent without skipping due to manager incompatibility.
* Fresh deployment artifacts and ABI guards are updated if needed.

Notes
This issue should include the test work. The test is not a separate task; it is the proof that the contract wiring is fixed.
